title: Genesis AI unveils GENE-26.5 robotics model with custom dexterous hands
subtitle: The Khosla-backed startup went full-stack — proprietary hardware plus foundation model — and demoed cooking, piano, and a Rubik's cube as evidence of human-scale manipulation.
author: AESOP AI Engine
body: Genesis AI, the robotics startup that emerged from stealth in July 2025 with a $105 million seed round co-led by Eclipse and Khosla Ventures, demoed its first foundation model on May 6: GENE-26.5. The system pairs a custom human-scale dexterous robotic hand — built in partnership with China's Wuji Tech — with a model trained on more than 200,000 hours of human hand data. CEO Zhou Xian says scaling that data lifted task success rates from 16.6% to 65.6%. The demo shows the hands cooking, preparing smoothies, playing piano, and solving a Rubik's cube.

Why it matters: most robotics foundation models so far have been hardware-agnostic — train on simulation and human videos, then transfer to whatever arm and gripper a customer has. Genesis bet that approach can't close the manipulation gap and built its own end-effector instead. The company also developed a wearable glove for data collection that it says is 100x cheaper than typical teleoperation rigs and 5x more efficient at gathering training data. That's a different theory of where the bottleneck lives — not in the model, but in the cost and quality of physical-world data.

This sits inside a competitive field that includes Physical Intelligence's Pi-series models, Nvidia's GR00T platform, Figure AI, and 1X. The full-stack-versus-platform debate inside robotics now mirrors what played out in autonomous vehicles a decade ago: the platform players bet on generality, and the vertically integrated players bet that hardware matters too much to abstract away. Both camps are about to get tested at scale.

Takeaway for learners: foundation models are leaving the language-and-image domain and meeting the messier constraints of physical action. If you want to build in this space, two skills compound — collecting good motion data and writing reward functions that don't reward shortcuts. The model architecture is becoming commoditized faster than either of those skills.

sources.title: Khosla-backed robotics startup Genesis AI has gone full stack, demo shows — TechCrunch
